behavior.business_purpose: The board's settings substrate: a flat (key,value) preference store plus the layered resolution that turns those strings into effective board behaviour — which agent provider/profile/model launches a workspace, whether and how aggressively a project auto-starts work, how the monitor balances feature-vs-bugfix, and what board config is portable. Its reason to exist is that one logical setting is rarely one row, so it is the layered resolver that collapses scattered inputs (Strategy Bullseye, global prefs, per-workspace override, legacy flags) into one effective answer every consumer agrees on. Also the system's most drift-prone surface, so several invariants exist purely to fail loudly instead of silently dropping/diverging.

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user via the Settings/Strategy UI

actors.name: monitor
actors.notes: in-process monitor + Conductor consume resolveStartPolicy / resolveMonitorTunables / selectProviderFromStrategy

actors.name: workspace-creator
actors.notes: POST /api/workspaces resolves provider/profile/model via the resolver chain

entry_points.observable: persists allowed keys (200 ok+applied) or rejects loudly (422) on unknown key or provider divergence; nothing persisted on divergence
returns only whitelisted/dynamic prefs; bootstrap returns settings+profiles+skills+tags in one round trip
reads/sets the active project id the divergence guard scopes against; stores arbitrary id without validation
enumerates selectable credential profiles per provider
reports global-pref vs Bullseye drift (passive banner)
live quota from tampermonkey-direct :8742; 503 when unavailable
exports portable board config (statuses+Bullseye+3 safe workflow prefs); imports with dryRun preview + non-fatal droppedKeys
pure resolvers consumed by workspace creation and monitors

behaviors.statement: A settings write persists every whitelisted (registry or dynamic) key and reports the applied set
preconditions: key is in SETTINGS_KEYS or matches a recognized dynamic prefix
behaviors.observable_outcome: 200 {ok:true, applied:[...]}; a later GET returns the saved value; upserts overwrite

behaviors.statement: An un-whitelisted key is rejected LOUDLY (422 + droppedKeys) rather than silently dropped; valid keys in the same write still apply
preconditions: body contains a key not in SETTINGS_KEYS and not a recognized dynamic key
behaviors.observable_outcome: 422 {ok:false, droppedKeys, applied}; the dropped key is NOT persisted, the valid key IS

behaviors.statement: A provider/profile write that would put global prefs out of sync with the active project's Bullseye is rejected (422) with nothing persisted — the enforced divergence invariant
preconditions: write touches provider or *_profile and would diverge
behaviors.observable_outcome: 422 {ok:false, divergence:{bullseyeProvider, settingsProvider,...}}; provider/profile prefs unchanged in DB

behaviors.statement: The divergence guard fires ONLY when the write touches a provider/profile key, and is a no-op when the active project has no Bullseye
preconditions: a pre-existing untouched drift exists, OR no Bullseye configured
behaviors.observable_outcome: an unrelated toggle save returns 200 despite drift; a provider write with no Bullseye returns 200 and persists

behaviors.statement: Provider/profile is resolved by a fixed precedence: explicit per-workspace override > legacy claudeProfile > Strategy Bullseye selection > global settings pref
preconditions: a workspace launch resolves its provider config from prefs
behaviors.observable_outcome: resolved {provider, profileName} follows precedence; an unknown provider string narrows to claude

behaviors.statement: Model is provider-scoped only: the retired global default_model is never read, and a model not belonging to the resolved provider is dropped to the provider default
preconditions: prefs may carry a stale global default_model and/or provider-scoped default_model_<provider>
behaviors.observable_outcome: only default_model_<provider> (or requestedModel) drives the model; global default_model has no effect; mismatch → undefined (provider default)

behaviors.statement: Start Mode is the single auto-start kill-switch: manual stops ALL auto-start, conductor forces in-process auto-start off, monitor enables it; an unset mode is derived from legacy flags (conductor never derived)
behaviors.observable_outcome: StartPolicy exposes autoStartUnblocked/postMergeCascade/backlogRefill/scheduledRuns booleans + source; manual → all false, conductor → all in-process false (scheduledRuns true)

behaviors.statement: A saved Strategy Bullseye derives the monitor's effective dials (active-agents target, backlog floor, max-new-starts, refill focus); bugfix-heavy weights yield bugfix-only refill; malformed/absent Bullseye falls back to legacy nudge prefs
preconditions: board_strategy_<id> JSON present (valid, malformed, or absent)
behaviors.observable_outcome: resolveMonitorTunables returns {tunables, source:'strategy'|'prefs'}; malformed JSON silently falls through to legacy with source='prefs'

behaviors.statement: Bullseye provider routing picks fill > throttle > fallback-only, skipping any policy blocked by live quota, and degrades gracefully (static order) when quota telemetry is missing
behaviors.observable_outcome: selectProviderFromStrategy returns the highest-priority non-quota-blocked policy; null when only fallback-only and allowFallback false; quota=null → ignores quota

behaviors.statement: A per-harness behavior knob resolves scoped (harness.<h>.<key>) > legacy flat key > per-harness default, treating empty scoped values as unset
preconditions: harness-scoped and/or legacy flat pref present
behaviors.observable_outcome: getHarnessBoolSetting returns the scoped value when set, else legacy, else the harness default

behaviors.statement: The Claude-specific --dangerously-skip-permissions flag is appended only for the Claude provider; other providers' args are left untouched
behaviors.observable_outcome: Claude agentArgs gain the flag; Copilot/Codex/Pi do not

behaviors.statement: The active-project preference can be read and set (stored as-is, no validation against the project list) and scopes the divergence guard
behaviors.observable_outcome: PUT echoes and persists projectId (incl. null/fake ids); GET returns it as both projectId and value

behaviors.statement: Board config export bundles statuses + Bullseye + the 3 safe workflow prefs and omits secrets/profiles; import validates shape, supports dryRun preview, drops disallowed keys non-fatally, and round-trips onto a fresh project
behaviors.observable_outcome: export JSON excludes provider/claude_profile; import 400 on bad shape, dryRun returns statusChanges without applying, evil keys ignored, round-trip reproduces statuses+strategy

behaviors.statement: The provider-divergence read endpoint reports whether global provider/profile prefs drift from a project's Bullseye (hasBullseye=false when none configured or projectId omitted)
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET returns {hasBullseye, bullseye*, settings*, diverged}; diverged=true on provider or profile mismatch, false when they agree or no Bullseye

behaviors.statement: Per-project/freeform dynamic keys are recognized by prefix (project-scoped requires a hex/UUID suffix; freeform requires any non-empty suffix), so unknowable project ids are accepted while garbage is rejected
behaviors.observable_outcome: valid project-scoped keys persist and read back; non-hex/empty suffixes are rejected; board_strategy_<id> is handled separately

behaviors.statement: Live quota telemetry is surfaced from the external tampermonkey-direct service and degrades to 503 (with empty providers) when that source is unavailable
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET returns quota data (200) or {error, providers:[]} with 503 when the external endpoint is down

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; permission dimension is N/A

transitions.trigger: Drive on / start_mode=monitor or derived from legacy flags

error_states.statement: PUT settings with an un-whitelisted key returns 422 + droppedKeys (valid keys still applied)
PUT settings with a diverging provider/profile returns 422 + divergence, nothing persisted
config import with an invalid shape returns 400
quota-usage returns 503 when the external telemetry source is down
INFERRED hazard: the write-time divergence guard fails OPEN on malformed Bullseye JSON (resolveProviderDivergence catches parse error → diverged:false), silently allowing the write it should block

external_integrations: git CLI (objective.md auto-commit on Bullseye save)

unknowns.question: Do the manual/conductor branches of resolveStartPolicy actually gate every auto-start path (post-merge cascade, backlog refill, crons) at runtime?
unknowns.why_unknown: the kill-switch claim is a monitor-orchestration concern; only the resolver's boolean outputs are observable here, and the manual/conductor branches are not directly asserted by any test

unknowns.question: Is the guard-fails-open-on-malformed-Bullseye hazard reachable from a real hand-edit, and does it actually let a diverging write through?
unknowns.why_unknown: no test exercises malformed-Bullseye + provider write; inferred from source try/catch returning diverged:false

unknowns.question: Does the objective.md auto-commit on a Bullseye save behave correctly through the PUT route (commit scoping, conductor vs non-conductor path)?
unknowns.why_unknown: service-level writeStrategyObjective/commitObjectiveFile are unit-tested, but the route-level updateStrategyObjectives wiring is not exercised end-to-end

gap.rationale: Only the mode='monitor' StartPolicy is asserted (via resolveProjectRuntimeConfig). The kill-switch semantics that justify this module's existence (decision 008) — manual → all four capability booleans false, conductor → in-process auto-start all false but scheduledRuns true, and the legacy-derivation branch (board_autodrive / auto_monitor+nudge_auto_start) with source='derived' — are NOT directly asserted by any test. buildDriveRuntimePreferencePatch tests the disable PATCH but not resolveStartPolicy's resulting booleans.

gap.rationale: No test exercises the GET /api/preferences/quota-usage route or its 503 graceful-degradation path. The quota selection LOGIC (isPolicyBlockedByQuota) is well covered, but the HTTP endpoint contract (200 shape vs 503 on external outage) is unverified. Confirmed live: the endpoint returns 503 when tampermonkey-direct :8742 is down.

top_gaps.rationale: The Start Mode kill-switch is this module's keystone (decision 008) and a documented multi-cycle-stall hazard, yet only mode='monitor' is asserted. Add direct resolveStartPolicy assertions: manual → autoStartUnblocked/postMergeCascade/backlogRefill/scheduledRuns all false; conductor → in-process all false but scheduledRuns true; derived branch yields mode+source='derived' from legacy board_autodrive / auto_monitor+nudge_auto_start. Pure-function test, near-zero exec/maint cost, high blast radius.

Inferred hazard: the write-time divergence guard fails OPEN on malformed Bullseye JSON (resolveProviderDivergence try/catch → diverged:false), silently permitting a provider/profile write the #903 invariant was meant to block. Add a negative test: active project with a corrupt board_strategy_<id> + a diverging provider write → currently 200 (drift persists). Either pins the current (surprising) behaviour or escalates it as a real defect — verify before asserting.

GET /api/preferences/quota-usage endpoint contract is untested (200 shape vs 503 on external :8742 outage). Inject a stub QuotaUsageProvider that throws → assert 503 + {providers:[]}; and one returning data → assert 200 shape. The provider is injectable, so this is cheap and stable; the live probe already confirms 503 on outage.
